body:
https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-sovereign/pull/2#issuecomment-4358605158

```
**Claude Opus 4.7:** (2 session segments)
1. peak request: 24.0K / 1M (2%) input tokens, 39.0K / 128K (30%) output tokens
2. peak request: 30.5K / 1M (3%) input tokens, 7.7K / 128K (6%) output tokens
```

Previously here output was correct, it showed 140-160k tokens per sub-session, now it looks like it shows maximum request, it was never a requirement. So I need to know total maximum input per sub-session, as to know how much context will be used if I restore this session at each moment. Will it fit at model context budget or not. So it is about peak input usage, and yet words `peak request:` must be removed.

For each sub-session (rename `session segments` to `sub-sessions`) between compaction events, we should get output like this:

```
1. 155.3K / 1M (2%) input tokens, 39.0K / 128K (30%) output tokens
2. 147.3K / 1M (3%) input tokens, 7.7K / 128K (6%) output tokens
```

We need only maximum total tokens per sub-session (input and output).

And totals for the model can contain cache writes and cache reads and actual total across all sub-sessions.


```
**Claude Haiku 4.5:**
- (28.5K new + 165.5K cache writes) input tokens, 6.7K / 64K (10%) output tokens
```

For haiku double check we sum all input tokens, cache writes, reads based on parent tool call.

Here also I expect not maximum request, but total of all request per sub-agent tool call to Haiku for example (with maximum if it is 200K or something):

```
- 58.5K/200K input tokens, 6.7K / 64K (10%) output tokens
```

And full total of Claude Haiku can contain full total of cache writes and reads.

Please make it simple, unified and easy to use, understand, and correctly calculate everything.

We should also support sub-session detection for Codex and other tools, with the same detailed style output as for Claude.


We need to download all logs and data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), in which we will reconstruct timeline/sequence of events, list of each and all requirements from the issue, find root causes of the each problem, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).

If there is not enough data to find actual root cause, add debug output and verbose mode if not present, that will allow us to find root cause on next iteration.

If issue related to any other repository/project, where we can report issues on GitHub, please do so. Each issue must contain reproducible examples, workarounds and suggestions for fix the issue in code.

Please plan and execute everything in a single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context autocompacts and you can continue indefinetely, do as much as possible in one go, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done.
title: Calculation was broken
